  applicable_facility_types varchar [note: 'Facility types where this form applies']
  applicable_service_lines varchar [note: 'Service lines where this form applies']
  approval_authority varchar [note: 'Authority that approved this form']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date form was approved']
  consent_category varchar [note: 'Category: treatment, research, HIE, HIPAA']
  consent_subcategory varchar [note: 'Subcategory within category']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User who created form template']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Record creation timestamp']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date form becomes effective']
  electronic_signature_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'True if electronic signature is allowed']
  expiration_date decimal(9,4) [note: 'Date form expires']
  form_checksum varchar [note: 'Checksum for form integrity verification']
  form_code varchar [note: 'Unique code for form template']
  form_document_url varchar [note: 'URL to form document']
  form_name varchar [note: 'Name of form template']
  form_purpose varchar [note: 'Purpose of consent form']
  form_status varchar [note: 'Status: draft, active, retired']
  interpreter_required_flag boolean [note: 'True if interpreter is required']
  irb_approval_date date [note: 'IRB approval date for research forms']
  irb_approval_number varchar [note: 'IRB approval number']
  irb_expiration_date decimal(9,4) [note: 'IRB approval expiration date']
  language_code varchar [note: 'Language of form']
  legal_representative_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'True if legal representative can sign']
  minimum_age_requirement varchar [note: 'Minimum age to consent']
  minor_assent_required_flag boolean [note: 'True if minor assent is required']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User who last modified form']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Last modification timestamp']
  reading_level varchar [note: 'Reading level of form']
  regulatory_basis varchar [note: 'Regulatory basis for form']
  retention_period_years varchar [note: 'Years to retain signed forms']
  revocation_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'True if consent can be revoked']
  revocation_instructions varchar [note: 'Instructions for revoking consent']
  scope_of_consent varchar [note: 'Scope of consent covered by form']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of form']
  witness_required_flag boolean [note: 'True if witness is required']

  Note: 'Consent form template library storing approved consent form versions with regulatory basis, IRB approval, language translations, and versioning. Supports form lifecycle management and compliance tracking.'
}

Table consent.hipaa_authorization {
  hipaa_authorization_id varchar [pk, note: 'Surrogate primary key']
  demographics_id varchar [note: 'FK to patient demographics']
  disclosing_org_provider_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.org_provider. Business justification: HIPAA authorizations identify the covered entity releasing PHI. disclosing_party_name and disclosing_party_npi are denormalized org_provider attributes. Normalizing via FK supports payer enrollment va']
  disclosing_party_npi_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.npi_registry. Business justification: HIPAA authorization compliance audits and breach investigations require identifying the disclosing party via their NPI registry record. disclosing_party_npi is a plain-text denormalization of the NPI ']
  form_template_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to consent.form_template. Business justification: hipaa_authorization currently stores form_version as a denormalized STRING. Adding form_template_id as a FK to form_template normalizes this relationship, allowing direct lookup of the approved form v']
  record_id varchar [note: 'FK to consent record']
  superseded_hipaa_authorization_id varchar [note: 'Self-FK to prior version']
  visit_id varchar [note: 'FK to visit']
  authorization_purpose varchar [note: 'Purpose of authorization']
  authorization_purpose_description varchar [note: 'Detailed purpose description']
  compensation_disclosure_flag boolean [note: 'True if compensation was disclosed']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Record creation timestamp']
  document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to authorization document']
  expiration_event decimal(9,4) [note: 'Event that triggers expiration']
  irb_approval_number varchar [note: 'IRB approval number']
  language_code varchar [note: 'Language code']
  last_updated_by varchar [note: 'User who last updated record']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Last update timestamp']
  mrn varchar [note: 'Patient MRN']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes']
  personal_representative_flag boolean [note: 'True if personal representative signed']
  personal_representative_name varchar [note: 'Name of personal representative']
  personal_representative_relationship varchar [note: 'Relationship to patient']
  phi_category varchar [note: 'Category of PHI to be disclosed']
  phi_description varchar [note: 'Description of PHI']
  recipient_address varchar [note: 'Address of recipient']
  recipient_name varchar [note: 'Name of recipient']
  recipient_organization varchar [note: 'Organization of recipient']
  redisclosure_statement varchar [note: 'Statement about redisclosure']
  right_to_revoke_statement varchar [note: 'Statement about right to revoke']
  signature_date date [note: 'Date of signature']
  signature_method varchar [note: 'Method of signature']
  signature_obtained_flag boolean [note: 'True if signature was obtained']
  witness_name varchar [note: 'Name of witness']
  witness_signature_date date [note: 'Date of witness signature']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User who created record']

  Note: 'HIPAA authorization for use and disclosure of PHI beyond treatment/payment/operations. Captures recipient, purpose, PHI categories, expiration, and right to revoke per 45 CFR 164.508.'
}

Table consent.treatment_consent {
  treatment_consent_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the treatment consent record. Primary key.']
  cpt_code_id varchar [note: 'The CPT or ICD-10-PCS code identifying the specific procedure or treatment being consented to. Applicable for procedure-specific consent types.']
  form_template_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to consent.form_template. Business justification: treatment_consent stores consent_form_number and consent_version as denormalized STRING fields referencing the consent form used. Adding form_template_id as a FK to form_template normalizes this relat']
  icd_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.icd_code. Business justification: Consent validity audits and clinical documentation require linking treatment consent to the ICD diagnosis that clinically justifies the procedure. Payers and accreditation bodies verify that consented']
  mpi_record_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the patient who is the subject of this treatment consent.']
  ndc_drug_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.ndc_drug. Business justification: Medication consent for high-alert drugs, chemotherapy, controlled substances, and investigational agents requires linking consent records to the specific NDC drug. Regulatory and accreditation standar']
  performing_org_provider_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.org_provider. Business justification: Treatment consent is facility-specific — patients consent to procedures at a named facility. Privileging, credentialing, and Joint Commission standards require linking consent to the performing org_pr']
  primary_treatment_clinician_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the provider who performed the clinical assessment of the patient\'s decision-making capacity.']
  record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to consent.record. Business justification: Treatment consent is a specialized type of consent record for medical procedures. Linking to the core record table enables unified consent management and reporting. Core consent lifecycle attributes (']
  superseded_treatment_consent_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on treatment_consent (superseded_treatment_consent_id)']
  tertiary_treatment_performing_provider_clinician_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the provider who will perform the procedure or treatment for which consent was obtained.']
  visit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical encounter during which this consent was obtained.']
  alternatives_documented varchar [note: 'Narrative documentation of alternative treatment options that were disclosed to and discussed with the patient or authorized representative.']
  benefits_documented varchar [note: 'Narrative documentation of the expected benefits of the procedure or treatment that were disclosed to and discussed with the patient or authorized representative.']
  capacity_determination varchar [note: 'Clinical determination of whether the patient has decision-making capacity to provide informed consent for the treatment or procedure.']
  consent_document_location varchar [note: 'Reference to the location or system where the signed consent document is stored (e.g., EMR document ID, scanned document repository path).']
  consent_method varchar [note: 'The method by which consent was obtained and documented (written signature, verbal consent documented by provider, electronic signature, telephonic consent with witness).']
  created_datetime timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this treatment consent record was first created in the system.']
  emergency_exception_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether treatment was provided under emergency exception provisions when informed consent could not be obtained due to the patient\'s condition and no authorized representative was available.']
  emergency_exception_justification varchar [note: 'Clinical documentation justifying why treatment was provided without informed consent under emergency exception provisions.']
  interpreter_language varchar [note: 'The language in which interpretation services were provided during the consent process.']
  interpreter_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the medical interpreter who facilitated the consent discussion.']
  interpreter_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a medical interpreter was required to facilitate the consent discussion due to language barriers or communication needs.']
  last_updated_datetime timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this treatment consent record was last modified in the system.']
  legal_representative_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the legal representative or authorized decision-maker who provided consent on behalf of the patient.']
  legal_representative_phone varchar [note: 'Contact phone number for the legal representative or authorized decision-maker.']
  legal_representative_relationship varchar [note: 'The legal or familial relationship of the representative to the patient (e.g., parent, legal guardian, healthcare proxy, durable power of attorney for healthcare, spouse, next of kin).']
  legal_representative_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a legal representative or authorized decision-maker was required to provide consent on behalf of the patient due to lack of capacity.']
  patient_questions_addressed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the patient or authorized representative had the opportunity to ask questions and all questions were addressed prior to obtaining consent.']
  patient_questions_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text documentation of specific questions asked by the patient or authorized representative and the responses provided.']
  revoked_by_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual (patient or authorized representative) who revoked the consent.']
  risks_documented varchar [note: 'Narrative documentation of the material risks of the procedure or treatment that were disclosed to and discussed with the patient or authorized representative.']
  special_instructions varchar [note: 'Any special instructions, limitations, or conditions specified by the patient or authorized representative as part of the consent (e.g., no blood products, specific anesthesia preferences).']
  vibe_mutation_marker varchar
  witness_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the witness who observed the consent process and signed the consent form.']
  witness_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a witness signature was required on the consent form per organizational policy or regulatory requirement.']
  witness_role varchar [note: 'The role or title of the witness (e.g., registered nurse, social worker, patient advocate).']

  Note: 'Master record for general and procedure-specific treatment consent obtained from patients or their authorized representatives prior to clinical care. Captures consent type (general treatment, surgical, anesthesia, blood transfusion, chemotherapy, ECT, restraint), procedure or treatment being consented to, risks and benefits documented, alternatives discussed, patient questions addressed, capacity determination, and legal representative details when patient lacks decision-making capacity. Distinct from HIPAA authorization — governs clinical care delivery.'
}

Table consent.revocation {
  revocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the consent revocation record. Primary key for the consent revocation entity.']
  mpi_record_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the patient who is revoking consent or on whose behalf consent is being revoked.']
  prior_revocation_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on revocation (prior_revocation_id)']
  record_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original consent record being revoked. Links to the parent consent that this revocation applies to.']
  audit_trail_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier linking to detailed audit trail records for all actions taken in response to this revocation, maintained for regulatory compliance.']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Internal notes regarding compliance considerations, special handling requirements, or regulatory reporting obligations related to this revocation.']
  data_access_restricted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether data access controls have been updated to restrict access based on the revoked consent.']
  data_access_restricted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when data access restrictions were implemented in response to the consent revocation.']
  disclosures_halted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether ongoing or future disclosures of Protected Health Information (PHI) have been halted as a result of this revocation.']
  disclosures_halted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when disclosure processes were halted in response to the consent revocation.']
  document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or file path to the scanned or electronic copy of the signed revocation document, if applicable.']
  effective_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the revocation became legally effective and operational systems began enforcing the withdrawal of consent. May differ from revocation_timestamp due to processing delays.']
  irrevocable_actions_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of any actions taken under the original consent that cannot be reversed or undone despite the revocation (e.g., data already shared with third parties, research already conducted).']
  legal_review_completed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether required legal review has been completed for this revocation.']
  legal_review_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this revocation requires legal review before being processed (e.g., complex partial revocations, disputes, or high-risk scenarios).']
  legal_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when legal review of the revocation was completed.']
  legal_reviewer_notes varchar [note: 'Confidential notes from legal counsel regarding the revocation review, including any special handling instructions or risk assessments.']
  method varchar [note: 'The method or channel through which the patient submitted the consent revocation (e.g., written form, verbal communication, patient portal, email, fax, in-person). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: written|verbal|electronic|portal|email|fax|in_person — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  notification_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether notification of the revocation was sent to relevant parties (providers, research teams, business associates, etc.).']
  notification_sent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when notifications regarding the consent revocation were sent to affected parties.']
  partial_revocation_details varchar [note: 'Free-text description of which specific portions of the consent are being revoked when revocation_scope is partial. Null when revocation is full.']
  patient_notification_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether confirmation notification was sent to the patient acknowledging receipt and processing of their revocation.']
  patient_notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when confirmation notification was sent to the patient regarding their consent revocation.']
  prior_disclosures_count varchar [note: 'The number of disclosures that occurred under the original consent prior to revocation and that cannot be undone or recalled.']
  prior_disclosures_summary varchar [note: 'Summary description of disclosures that occurred prior to revocation and cannot be undone, maintained for legal audit trail and patient notification.']
  reason varchar [note: 'Optional free-text explanation provided by the patient describing why they are revoking consent. May be blank if patient chose not to provide a reason.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized categorical code representing the reason for consent revocation, used for reporting and analytics.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this consent revocation record was first created in the data warehouse or lakehouse.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this consent revocation record was last updated in the data warehouse or lakehouse.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation of why the revocation was rejected, if applicable (e.g., invalid signature, unauthorized submitter, incomplete documentation).']
  revocation_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the patient submitted the consent revocation. This is the effective date from which the consent is considered withdrawn.']
  revocation_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier or tracking number assigned to this consent revocation for reference and audit purposes.']
  revocation_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the consent revocation within the organization\'s workflow (e.g., pending review, processed and effective, rejected due to invalid submission).']
  revocation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the consent revocation was submitted or recorded in the system. Provides exact temporal audit trail for legal compliance.']
  scope varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the revocation applies to the entire consent (full) or only specific portions of the consent (partial).']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier for this revocation record in the source operational system.']
  vibe_mutation_marker varchar
  witness_name varchar [note: 'Name of the witness who was present when the revocation was submitted, if applicable (e.g., for verbal revocations or in-person submissions).']
  witness_signature_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a witness signature was obtained on the revocation document.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of every consent revocation submitted by a patient or their authorized representative. Captures revocation date and time, revocation method (written, verbal, electronic), reason for revocation (if provided), scope of revocation (full or partial), actions taken in response (notifications sent, data access restricted, disclosures halted), and any disclosures that occurred prior to revocation that cannot be undone. Provides the legal audit trail required by HIPAA and state law for consent withdrawal.'
}

Table consent.npp_acknowledgment {
  npp_acknowledgment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the NPP acknowledgment transaction. Primary key.']
  acknowledging_org_provider_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.org_provider. Business justification: HIPAA requires covered entities to document NPP acknowledgment at each facility location (45 CFR 164.520). Multi-facility compliance reporting and OCR audit responses require knowing which org_provide']
  adt_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to encounter.adt_event. Business justification: HIPAA requires NPP acknowledgment at first service delivery, which operationally occurs at the admit ADT event. Linking npp_acknowledgment to the specific adt_event captures the registration workflow ']
  mpi_record_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the patient who acknowledged or was presented with the NPP.']
  portal_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to patient.portal_account. Business justification: HIPAA compliance audit trails for digitally-obtained NPP acknowledgments require linking to the specific portal account session. patient_portal_session_code is a denormalized reference to portal_accou']
  primary_previous_npp_acknowledgment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the previous NPP acknowledgment record for this patient, if this is a re-acknowledgment following a material change.']
  record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to consent.consent_record. Business justification: npp_acknowledgment is the only consent transactional product in this domain that lacks a record_id FK to consent_record. Every other consent product (hipaa_authorization, treatment_consent, revocation']
  registration_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to patient.registration_event. Business justification: HIPAA Privacy Rule requires documenting that NPP acknowledgment was obtained at first service delivery. Linking npp_acknowledgment to registration_event enables compliance reporting on whether NPP was']
  visit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the clinical encounter during which the NPP acknowledgment was obtained, if applicable.']
  accessibility_accommodation varchar [note: 'Description of any accessibility accommodations provided (e.g., large print, Braille, audio recording, sign language interpreter).']
  acknowledgment_date date [note: 'Date on which the patient acknowledged receipt of the NPP.']
  acknowledgment_location varchar [note: 'Physical or virtual location where the acknowledgment was obtained. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: registration_desk|patient_room|emergency_department|outpatient_clinic|patient_portal|home|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  acknowledgment_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the patient provided acknowledgment (paper signature, electronic signature, patient portal click-through, email confirmation, kiosk acceptance, verbal acknowledgment documented by staff).']
  acknowledgment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the NPP acknowledgment transaction. Acknowledged indicates patient signed or electronically accepted; unable_to_obtain indicates good-faith effort was made but acknowledgment could not be secured per regulatory allowance.']
  acknowledgment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the patient acknowledged receipt of the NPP, particularly important for electronic acknowledgments.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this acknowledgment record was first created in the system.']
  delivery_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the NPP was delivered to the patient (paper handout, electronic via patient portal, email, kiosk, verbal explanation).']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Type of device used for electronic acknowledgment (desktop computer, mobile phone, tablet, kiosk).']
  good_faith_effort_documentation varchar [note: 'Free-text documentation of the good-faith effort made to obtain acknowledgment when it could not be secured.']
  good_faith_effort_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why acknowledgment could not be obtained despite good-faith effort, as permitted under 45 CFR 164.520(c)(2)(ii).']
  interpreter_used boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an interpreter was used to explain the NPP to the patient.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address from which the electronic acknowledgment was submitted, for audit trail purposes.']
  is_first_service_acknowledgment boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this acknowledgment was obtained at the first service delivery as required by 45 CFR 164.520(c)(1).']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-2 three-letter language code indicating the language in which the NPP was provided to the patient.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this acknowledgment record was last modified.']
  material_change_acknowledgment boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this acknowledgment was obtained following a material change to the NPP, requiring re-acknowledgment.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes or comments regarding the NPP acknowledgment transaction.']
  representative_authority_documented boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the authority of a personal representative to act on behalf of the patient was documented and verified.']
  retention_expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which this acknowledgment record may be destroyed per retention policy.']
  retention_period_years varchar [note: 'Number of years this acknowledgment record must be retained per HIPAA record retention requirements (minimum 6 years from creation or last effective date).']
  revocation_date date [note: 'Date on which the acknowledgment was revoked or voided, if applicable.']
  revocation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why the acknowledgment was revoked or voided (e.g., administrative error, duplicate entry, patient request).']
  signature_captured boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a patient or representative signature was captured as part of the acknowledgment.']
  signature_type varchar [note: 'Type of signature captured (wet ink on paper, electronic stylus, digital certificate-based, biometric, or none if acknowledgment was obtained without signature).']
  signer_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual who signed the acknowledgment (patient or authorized representative).']
  signer_relationship varchar [note: 'Relationship of the signer to the patient (self if patient signed, or representative role). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: self|parent|legal_guardian|power_of_attorney|healthcare_proxy|personal_representative|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of this acknowledgment record in the source system.']
  vibe_mutation_marker varchar

  Note: 'Transactional record of patient acknowledgment of receipt of the organization\'s HIPAA Notice of Privacy Practices (NPP). Captures acknowledgment date, delivery method (paper, electronic, patient portal), NPP version acknowledged, patient or representative signature, and documentation of good-faith efforts when acknowledgment could not be obtained. Distinct from compliance.notice_of_privacy_practices which tracks the NPP document itself — this tracks the patient-level acknowledgment transaction required by 45 CFR 164.520.'
}

Table consent.restriction_request {
  restriction_request_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the patient consent restriction request record. Primary key.']
  icd_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.icd_code. Business justification: HIPAA and 42 CFR Part 2 allow patients to restrict disclosure of PHI related to specific diagnoses (HIV, substance abuse, mental health). Linking restriction_request to ICD code enables systems to enf']
  insurance_coverage_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to patient.insurance_coverage. Business justification: HIPAA permits patients paying out-of-pocket to request disclosure restrictions to their health plan. Attribute out_of_pocket_payment_verified documents this business requirement. Link enables verifica']
  mpi_record_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the patient who submitted the restriction request. Links to the patient master record.']
  receiving_org_provider_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.org_provider. Business justification: HIPAA 164.522 restriction requests are submitted to and decided by a specific covered entity (org_provider). organization_decision field confirms the org makes the ruling. Compliance tracking, system_']
  record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to consent.consent_record. Business justification: Consent restriction requests are patient requests to restrict PHI use beyond standard consent. While some restriction requests are general (not tied to specific consent), many relate to a specific con']
  superseded_restriction_request_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on restriction_request (superseded_restriction_request_id)']
  visit_id varchar [note: 'The encounter during which the restriction request was submitted, if applicable. Links to the encounter master record.']
  audit_trail_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier to the detailed audit trail of all actions taken on this restriction request, including reviews, decisions, notifications, and enforcement activities.']
  compliance_review_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent compliance review of this restriction request to ensure ongoing adherence to HIPAA and HITECH requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this restriction request record was first created in the system. Used for audit and data lineage purposes.']
  decision_date date [note: 'The date the covered entity made the decision to accept or deny the restriction request.']
  decision_made_by varchar [note: 'The name or role of the individual or committee who made the decision on the restriction request (e.g., Privacy Officer, HIM Director).']
  decision_rationale varchar [note: 'The business and regulatory rationale for the organization\'s decision to accept or deny the restriction request. Required for audit and patient communication.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which the restriction becomes effective and must be honored by the covered entity.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which the restriction expires, if applicable. Null indicates an indefinite restriction until revoked by the patient or terminated by the organization per regulatory provisions.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this restriction request record was last modified. Used for audit and data lineage purposes.']
  mrn varchar [note: 'The patient\'s medical record number as recorded at the time of the restriction request. Provides business-level traceability.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes or comments related to the restriction request, including patient concerns, special handling instructions, or escalation details.']
  operational_instructions varchar [note: 'Detailed operational instructions for clinical, billing, and administrative staff on how to honor the restriction across Electronic Health Record (EHR), Revenue Cycle Management (RCM), Health Information Exchange (HIE), and other systems.']
  organization_decision varchar [note: 'The covered entity\'s decision on whether to accept or deny the restriction request. HITECH out-of-pocket restrictions must be accepted; other restrictions are discretionary.']
  out_of_pocket_payment_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the patient\'s claim of out-of-pocket payment has been verified for HITECH mandatory restrictions. True if verified, False if not applicable or not verified.']
  patient_notification_date date [note: 'The date the patient was notified of the organization\'s decision on the restriction request or any subsequent changes to the restriction status.']
  patient_notification_method varchar [note: 'The method used to notify the patient of the decision or status change. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mail|email|patient_portal|phone|in_person|secure_message|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  payment_verification_date date [note: 'The date the out-of-pocket payment was verified for HITECH mandatory restrictions, if applicable.']
  request_date date [note: 'The date the patient submitted the restriction request to the covered entity.']
  request_method varchar [note: 'The method by which the patient submitted the restriction request. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: written_form|verbal|patient_portal|email|fax|mail|in_person|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  request_number varchar [note: 'Business-assigned unique identifier for the restriction request, used for tracking and reference in correspondence and workflows.']
  request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time the restriction request was received and recorded in the system.']
  requesting_party_relationship varchar [note: 'The relationship of the individual submitting the restriction request to the patient, if not the patient themselves.']
  restricted_phi_category varchar [note: 'The category of Protected Health Information (PHI) the patient is requesting to restrict. Used for operational filtering and enforcement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: all_phi|diagnosis_codes|procedure_codes|lab_results|radiology_reports|medication_records|mental_health_records|substance_abuse_records|genetic_information|other — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  restricted_purpose varchar [note: 'The purpose of use or disclosure that the patient is requesting to restrict (e.g., restrict disclosure for payment purposes to a specific payer). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: treatment|payment|healthcare_operations|research|public_health|all_purposes|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  restricted_recipient_name varchar [note: 'The specific name of the individual, organization, or health plan to whom the restriction applies, if identified by the patient.']
  restricted_recipient_type varchar [note: 'The type of recipient or entity to whom the patient is requesting disclosure restrictions apply. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: health_plan|family_member|employer|specific_provider|health_information_exchange|research_entity|public_health_authority|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  restriction_scope varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the scope of the requested restriction, including specific Protected Health Information (PHI) data elements, recipients, purposes, or time periods the patient wishes to restrict.']
  restriction_status varchar [note: 'The current lifecycle status of the restriction. Active restrictions must be enforced across all clinical and administrative systems.']
  restriction_type varchar [note: 'The category of restriction requested by the patient. HITECH out-of-pocket payer restriction is mandatory per 45 CFR 164.522(a)(1)(vi); other types are discretionary.']
  revocation_date date [note: 'The date the patient revoked the restriction request, if applicable. Patients may revoke restrictions at any time per HIPAA.']
  revocation_method varchar [note: 'The method by which the patient revoked the restriction, if applicable. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: written_form|verbal|patient_portal|email|fax|mail|in_person|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  supporting_documentation_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or location of supporting documentation submitted with the restriction request (e.g., signed forms, proof of out-of-pocket payment for HITECH restrictions).']
  system_enforcement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the restriction is actively enforced through automated system controls (True) or requires manual staff intervention (False).']
  termination_date date [note: 'The date the covered entity terminated the restriction, if applicable. Organizations may terminate restrictions under specific circumstances per HIPAA.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'The reason the covered entity terminated the restriction, including regulatory justification and patient notification details.']
  vibe_mutation_marker varchar

  Note: 'Master record for patient requests to restrict uses and disclosures of their PHI beyond HIPAA\'s standard permissions. Captures restriction type (restrict disclosure to specific payer when patient paid out-of-pocket per HITECH, restrict sharing with family members, restrict specific data types), requested restriction scope, organization\'s decision to accept or deny the restriction, effective date, and operational instructions for honoring the restriction across clinical systems. Governed by HITECH Act amendment to HIPAA 45 CFR 164.522.'
}

Table consent.disclosure_log {
  disclosure_log_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the consent disclosure log entry. Primary key for the consent disclosure log product.']
  claim_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to claim.claim. Business justification: HIPAA accounting of disclosures (45 CFR §164.528) requires logging PHI disclosures triggered by claim submissions to payers. The disclosure_log must reference the specific claim that caused the disclo']
  disclosing_clinician_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.clinician. Business justification: HIPAA accounting of disclosures (45 CFR 164.528) requires identifying which clinician released PHI. Compliance audits and breach investigations depend on this link. disclosure_initiated_by_role confir']
  disclosing_org_provider_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.org_provider. Business justification: Multi-facility HIPAA disclosure accounting requires knowing which org_provider location released PHI. Facility-level compliance reporting, OIG audits, and breach notification workflows all depend on i']
  disclosure_record_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the consent or authorization record under which this disclosure was made. Links to the consent product.']
  hipaa_authorization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to consent.hipaa_authorization. Business justification: PHI disclosures are frequently made under specific HIPAA authorizations. The disclosure_log currently has authorization_reference (STRING) which should be replaced with a proper FK to hipaa_authorizat']
  mpi_record_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the patient whose Protected Health Information (PHI) was disclosed. Links to the patient master product.']
  parent_disclosure_log_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on disclosure_log (related_disclosure_log_id)']
  primary_disclosure_consent_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to consent.consent_record. Business justification: The consent_record product description explicitly states it is \'Referenced by disclosure_consent_record_id\', and disclosure_log records every PHI disclosure made under a patient consent or authorizati']
  recipient_npi_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.npi_registry. Business justification: HIPAA accounting of disclosures requires identifying NPI-registered recipients (covered entities, business associates). Linking disclosure_log to npi_registry enables automated compliance reports and ']
  visit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical encounter or visit associated with this disclosure, if applicable. Links to the encounter product.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this disclosure log record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage.']
  department_code varchar [note: 'The code or identifier of the department or unit within the facility that made the disclosure. Supports departmental accountability and reporting.']
  disclosure_date date [note: 'The date on which the Protected Health Information (PHI) disclosure occurred. Required for HIPAA Accounting of Disclosures per 45 CFR 164.528.']
  disclosure_initiated_by varchar [note: 'The name or identifier of the individual or system that initiated the disclosure. Supports audit trail and accountability.']
  disclosure_initiated_by_role varchar [note: 'The role or job title of the individual who initiated the disclosure, such as physician, nurse, HIM specialist, or system administrator. Supports role-based access auditing.']
  disclosure_method varchar [note: 'The method or channel by which the Protected Health Information (PHI) was disclosed. Supports security analysis and breach risk assessment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: electronic|paper|verbal|fax|secure_email|hie|api|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  disclosure_notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or context regarding the disclosure. Provides supplementary information for audit and compliance review.']
  disclosure_purpose varchar [note: 'The stated purpose or reason for the Protected Health Information (PHI) disclosure. Required for HIPAA Accounting of Disclosures per 45 CFR 164.528.']
  disclosure_purpose_category varchar [note: 'The standardized category of the disclosure purpose. Enables aggregation and reporting of disclosures by purpose type for compliance and analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: treatment|payment|operations|research|public_health|legal|patient_request|court_order|law_enforcement|other — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  disclosure_status varchar [note: 'The current status of the disclosure transaction. Tracks the lifecycle state of the disclosure event.']
  disclosure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the Protected Health Information (PHI) disclosure occurred. Provides granular audit trail for compliance and security investigations.']
  is_accounting_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this disclosure must be included in the HIPAA Accounting of Disclosures report provided to patients upon request. Non-TPO disclosures are subject to accounting per 45 CFR 164.528.']
  is_tpo_disclosure boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the disclosure was made for Treatment, Payment, or Operations (TPO) purposes. TPO disclosures are exempt from HIPAA Accounting of Disclosures requirements per 45 CFR 164.528.']
  legal_basis varchar [note: 'The legal or regulatory basis that permitted or required the disclosure, such as court order, subpoena, public health reporting requirement, or patient authorization.']
  minimum_necessary_applied boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the minimum necessary standard was applied to limit the Protected Health Information (PHI) disclosed to only what was reasonably necessary for the stated purpose, as required by 45 CFR 164.502(b).']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'The user ID or system identifier that last modified this disclosure log record. Supports audit trail and accountability.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this disclosure log record was last modified. Supports audit trail and change tracking.']
  patient_notification_date date [note: 'The date on which the patient was notified of the disclosure, if notification was required. Supports compliance tracking.']
  patient_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the patient must be notified of this disclosure under HIPAA or organizational policy. Certain disclosures require patient notification.']
  phi_elements_disclosed varchar [note: 'Description of the specific Protected Health Information (PHI) elements or data categories that were disclosed. Required for HIPAA Accounting of Disclosures.']
  recipient_address varchar [note: 'The mailing or physical address of the recipient to whom the Protected Health Information (PHI) was disclosed. Required for HIPAA Accounting of Disclosures.']
  recipient_identifier varchar [note: 'The unique identifier for the recipient, such as National Provider Identifier (NPI), Tax ID, or organizational identifier. Enables precise tracking of disclosure recipients.']
  recipient_name varchar [note: 'The name of the individual, organization, or entity to whom the Protected Health Information (PHI) was disclosed. Required for HIPAA Accounting of Disclosures.']
  recipient_type varchar [note: 'The category of recipient to whom the Protected Health Information (PHI) was disclosed. Supports classification and reporting of disclosure patterns. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: individual|organization|government_agency|health_plan|clearinghouse|business_associate|research_institution|public_health_authority|law_enforcement|other — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  system_source varchar [note: 'The name or identifier of the source system or application that recorded the disclosure event, such as Epic EHR, Cerner Millennium, or HIE platform.']
  vibe_mutation_marker varchar
  created_by varchar [note: 'The user ID or system identifier that created this disclosure log record. Supports audit trail and accountability.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of every PHI disclosure made under a patient consent or authorization, providing the accounting of disclosures required by HIPAA. Captures disclosure date, recipient identity and type, purpose of disclosure, PHI elements disclosed, consent or authorization reference, and whether the disclosure was for TPO (exempt from accounting) or non-TPO (subject to accounting). Enables generation of the HIPAA Accounting of Disclosures report provided to patients upon request per 45 CFR 164.528.'
}

Table consent.record {
  record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for consent_record']
  form_template_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the consent form template version presented to the patient, ensuring traceability to the exact wording and disclosures the patient agreed to.']
  issuing_org_provider_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.org_provider. Business justification: Consent records are issued under a specific facility\'s authority and retention policies. Multi-facility health systems require facility-level consent tracking for state regulatory compliance, retentio']
  mpi_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to patient.mpi_record. Business justification: Patient consent history reporting, HIPAA audit trails, and compliance dashboards all require knowing which patient owns a consent_record without traversing through visit or child tables. Every consent']
  obtaining_clinician_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.clinician. Business justification: Joint Commission and CMS Conditions of Participation require documenting which clinician obtained informed consent. Credentialing reviews, malpractice defense, and consent validity audits depend on th']
  primary_superseded_by_consent_record_id varchar [note: 'Self-reference to the consent record that replaces this one when a patient re-consents or updates their authorization, preserving consent version lineage.']
  visit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical encounter during which the consent was obtained, when applicable, supporting episode-of-care context for treatment consent.']
  applies_to_minor boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the consent pertains to a minor patient, triggering guardian-consent and age-of-majority transition handling.']
  can_revoke boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the patient retains the right to revoke this consent, noting that some research and prior-disclosure consents are not fully revocable.']
  consent_category varchar [note: 'Higher-level business grouping of the consent that drives downstream access control and policy application, including specially-protected categories such as behavioral health and substance use.']
  consent_direction varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the consent grants (permit) or restricts (deny) the described activity, supporting opt-in versus opt-out semantics for HIE and data sharing.']
  consent_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known human-readable business reference number for the consent record, used on documents and in patient communications.']
  consent_record_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the consent record, driving whether the consent is enforceable for data access, treatment, or disclosure decisions.']
  consent_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the fundamental kind of consent captured, distinguishing treatment consent, research consent, data sharing authorization, HIPAA authorization, HIE opt-in/opt-out, and telehealth consent.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this consent record was first captured in the system of record.']
  document_uri varchar [note: 'Storage location reference for the scanned or electronic signed consent document, enabling retrieval of the original legal artifact.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the consent becomes legally binding and enforceable for the described activities.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the consent automatically lapses; nullable for consents that remain in effect until explicitly revoked.']
  expiration_event varchar [note: 'Description of an event-based expiration condition (e.g., end of research study) used when a consent terminates on an event rather than a fixed date, per HIPAA authorization requirements.']
  includes_sensitive_data boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the consent covers specially-protected sensitive information such as substance use, behavioral health, or HIV status requiring heightened controls.']
  interpreter_used boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an interpreter assisted the patient during the consent process, supporting language-access compliance documentation.']
  is_legally_authorized_representative boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the consent was provided by a legally authorized representative on behalf of the patient, requiring documentation of authority.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 language code in which the consent form was presented and signed, documenting that the patient consented in a language they understand.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this consent record was most recently modified, supporting change auditing and data lineage.']
  purpose_of_use varchar [note: 'Authorized purpose for which the protected health information may be used or disclosed under this consent, aligned with HIPAA permitted-purpose categories.']
  scope_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the specific information categories, purposes, and recipients covered by this consent, defining the permitted use boundary.']
  signature_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the patient signature or assent was captured, supporting validity assessment and e-signature compliance auditing.']
  signed_date date [note: 'Date the patient or authorized representative signed and executed the consent, establishing the principal real-world consent event.']
  signer_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the individual who signed the consent, which may be the patient or a legally authorized representative.']
  signer_relationship varchar [note: 'Relationship of the signer to the patient, identifying whether consent was self-given or provided by an authorized representative.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Status of the verification process confirming the validity and completeness of the consent before it is treated as enforceable.']
  withdrawal_date date [note: 'Date on which the patient revoked or withdrew the consent; nullable while consent remains active.']
  withdrawal_method varchar [note: 'Channel through which the patient communicated their revocation, supporting validity verification of the withdrawal.']
  withdrawal_reason varchar [note: 'Documented reason the patient provided for revoking the consent, supporting audit and patient-rights reporting.']
  witness_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the witness who attested to the patient signature, required for certain treatment and research consents.']
  witness_signed_date date [note: 'Date the witness signed and attested to the consent execution.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for consent_record. Referenced by disclosure_consent_record_id.'
}
